text:
PROTOCOL-MANDATED RESTAGING CT CHEST/ABDOMEN/PELVIS WITH CONTRAST AND MRI BRAIN WITH AND WITHOUT GADOLINIUM

CLINICAL INDICATION: 64-year-old female with stage IV EGFR L858R-mutant lung adenocarcinoma, currently enrolled on phase II clinical trial (investigational EGFR/MET bispecific antibody plus oral SOS1 inhibitor). Prior history of osimertinib first-line therapy with acquired T790M resistance, subsequent SRS to three brain metastases, and four cycles of carboplatin/pemetrexed followed by pemetrexed maintenance. Currently completing cycle 2 of clinical trial therapy. Protocol-mandated restaging study.

---

CT CHEST/ABDOMEN/PELVIS WITH IV CONTRAST

TECHNIQUE: Multidetector CT of the chest, abdomen, and pelvis obtained in the portal venous phase following administration of 100 mL of iohexol 350 IV contrast. 3 mm axial reconstructions with coronal and sagittal reformats. Comparison: Prior CT from approximately 8 weeks ago (pemetrexed maintenance/pre-trial baseline).

FINDINGS:

Chest:
Lungs: The right lower lobe mass, previously measuring 2.3 cm in longest axial dimension on the comparison study, now measures approximately 1.7 cm. The lesion maintains its irregular, spiculated morphology but demonstrates interval decrease in size. No central cavitation. There is no associated endobronchial component appreciated. Previously noted bilateral small pulmonary nodules — multiple bilateral subcentimeter nodules seen on imaging 5-6 months ago — are no longer identified on today's examination. No new pulmonary nodules or masses. No areas of consolidation or ground-glass opacity to suggest pneumonitis. Lung windows demonstrate no new nodular densities.

Mediastinum/Lymph Nodes: Previously described right paratracheal (station 4R) lymphadenopathy, which had recurred at 1.2 cm on imaging at time of osimertinib progression, is now markedly diminished — the 4R nodal cluster measures approximately 0.7 cm in short axis, at or near the upper limit of normal. Subcarinal region (station 7) without pathologically enlarged nodes. No new mediastinal or hilar adenopathy identified. Prevascular and aortopulmonary window lymph nodes within normal size limits.

Pleura/Pericardium: No pleural effusion bilaterally. No pericardial effusion. No pneumothorax. Prior SRS-treated sites are not directly assessable on CT.

Heart and Great Vessels: Heart size normal. Aorta and great vessels unremarkable for age. No significant atherosclerotic calcification.

Chest Wall/Bones: No lytic or sclerotic osseous lesions in the ribs or visualized thoracic spine. Soft tissues unremarkable.

Abdomen:
Liver: The liver is normal in size and attenuation. No focal hepatic lesions identified. No biliary ductal dilatation. Gallbladder is present without stones or wall thickening. Portal vein patent.

Spleen: Normal size and attenuation. No focal lesions.

Pancreas: Unremarkable. No pancreatic ductal dilatation or focal mass.

Kidneys: Bilateral kidneys are symmetric in size and enhancement. No hydronephrosis. No suspicious renal masses. Bilateral small subcentimeter hypodensities consistent with simple cysts.

Adrenal Glands: The right adrenal gland contains a nodule previously measuring 1.1 cm at the pre-trial baseline; today this nodule measures approximately 0.7 cm with preserved ovoid morphology and decreased conspicuity relative to prior studies. CT attenuation characteristics remain non-specific, without convincing washout characteristics to characterize as adenoma. The left adrenal gland is normal in size and appearance.

Gastrointestinal: Small and large bowel loops are unremarkable. No bowel wall thickening. No mesenteric adenopathy. No free intraperitoneal air.

Retroperitoneum/Lymph Nodes: No retroperitoneal, aortocaval, or pelvic lymphadenopathy.

Pelvis:
Urinary bladder is decompressed but without obvious wall abnormality. Uterus and ovaries not individually identified but no pelvic mass. No free pelvic fluid. Bilateral iliac vessels patent.

Bones (Abdomen/Pelvis): No suspicious lytic or blastic lesions in the visualized lumbar spine, sacrum, or pelvis.

---

MRI BRAIN WITH AND WITHOUT GADOLINIUM

TECHNIQUE: MRI brain obtained at 3 Tesla using standard brain tumor protocol including pre- and post-gadolinium T1-weighted sequences, T2, FLAIR, diffusion-weighted imaging, and susceptibility-weighted imaging. 2 mm contiguous slices. Gadolinium dose: 0.1 mmol/kg gadobutrol IV. Comparison: Prior MRI brain from approximately 10 weeks ago (post-SRS, at time of carboplatin/pemetrexed cycle 3 restaging).

FINDINGS:

Previously Treated Lesions:

Right cerebellar hemisphere: Previously known SRS-treated lesion, which measured 0.6 cm with central necrosis on the comparison study, today measures approximately 0.5 cm in greatest dimension. There is a persistent ring of T2/FLAIR signal abnormality surrounding the treated site, consistent with treatment-related edema and post-radiation change. The central component demonstrates susceptibility artifact on SWI, compatible with hemosiderin deposition/necrotic debris. No nodular or irregular enhancement to suggest viable progressive tumor at this site. Overall appearance is consistent with continued treatment effect.

Left frontal lobe: Previously treated SRS site, measuring 0.4 cm on the comparison study, is now barely perceptible — approximately 0.3 cm or less, near the limits of spatial resolution. Mild surrounding FLAIR signal change persists but is unchanged. Post-gadolinium sequences show faint rim-like enhancement in keeping with post-SRS changes without convincing nodular or solid enhancing component.

Right parietal lobe: Previously treated SRS site, which measured 0.3 cm on the comparison study, demonstrates minimal residual T1 signal change. No discrete enhancing nodule appreciated on today's study. The surrounding FLAIR signal abnormality has largely resolved.

New Lesions: No new enhancing intracranial lesions identified. Careful inspection of the posterior fossa, brainstem, cerebellopontine angles, and leptomeningeal surfaces reveals no suspicious nodular enhancement.

Leptomeninges: No leptomeningeal enhancement. The sulci and cisterns enhance uniformly and symmetrically without nodular deposits.

Brain Parenchyma: No new areas of restricted diffusion to suggest acute infarct or tumor infiltration. White matter appears normal. Ventricles are normal in size and configuration. No midline shift. No hydrocephalus. Sella and parasellar regions unremarkable.

Calvarium/Scalp: No osseous lesions of the calvarium. Scalp soft tissues unremarkable.

---

IMPRESSION:

1. The right lower lobe primary lung mass has decreased in size, now approximately 1.7 cm compared to 2.3 cm on the prior comparison study. The previously noted bilateral small pulmonary nodules have resolved and are no longer visualized.

2. The right adrenal nodule has similarly decreased in size from 1.1 cm to approximately 0.7 cm, and appears less conspicuous than on prior studies.

3. The previously enlarged right paratracheal (4R) lymph node cluster has diminished markedly and now approximates normal size limits.

4. No new sites of thoracic, abdominal, or pelvic disease identified.

5. MRI brain: The three previously SRS-treated intracranial lesions show continued progressive volume reduction and are characterized by post-treatment change including hemosiderin deposition and resolving edema, without imaging features to suggest viable or recurrent tumor at any of the three sites. No new intracranial lesions. No leptomeningeal disease.

Overall, findings are consistent with an encouraging response across all known sites of disease since initiating clinical trial therapy, including both the chest and the adrenal metastasis, and continued favorable evolution of the previously treated brain metastases.
